key_insight: AA-ViT synthesizes contrast-enhanced brain MRI from routine pre-contrast scans while better preserving anatomical and tumour boundaries.
clinical_relevance: If validated further, the approach could reduce reliance on gadolinium contrast agents, cut scan time and cost, and improve access for patients who cannot safely receive contrast.
method: Uses a vision transformer designed to be anatomically aware.
Guides synthesis with both structural and frequency information.
Inputs T1, T2, and FLAIR MRI to generate synthetic post-contrast images.
evidence_signal: On the BraTS 2021 dataset, the method outperformed state-of-the-art approaches on PSNR and SSIM and received a mean clinical rating of 3.94/5 from three neuroradiologists and one neurosurgeon across 19 cases.
watch_item: The clinical evaluation was small and preliminary, so broader validation is needed before real-world use.
paper_title: AA-ViT: Anatomically Aware Vision Transformer with Structural and Frequency Guidance for Contrast Enhanced Brain MRI Synthesis
